traceReason: fix/ branches require auditable traces for repository-affecting work
taskSummary: Apply the nine hardening fixes identified against main on a new branch ready for PR review.

bundlesSkipped.reason: No UI/content or GOV.UK component behaviour was changed directly.
No OpenAI API or AI safety implementation was changed.
No MCP or agent-tooling contract was changed.
No Airtable API request/response contract was changed directly.

implementationSummary: Legacy Pages Function proxy now uses allowlisted credentialed CORS, strips upstream CORS, adds CSRF confirmation to proxied mutations and returns generic upstream errors.
Advanced Pages Worker proxy diagnostics are hidden unless RESEARCHOPS_PROXY_DIAGNOSTICS_ENABLED=true.
Production persistent invocation logging is disabled and log sampling is reduced.
Mural OAuth state is signed and token storage is keyed by authenticated ResearchOps user id.
Mural endpoints now receive Worker auth context and anonymous uid fallback is removed from journal sync payload parsing.
Deploy hook route requires deployment.trigger permission and uses constant-time token comparison without returning Cloudflare hook response text.
Diagnostic endpoints are disabled unless RESEARCHOPS_DIAGNOSTICS_ENABLED=true.
Passwordless preview config uses provisioned Cloudflare KV/D1 bindings and a tightened allowlist.
Security workflow now runs on relevant PR changes with Node 22 and package engine metadata is aligned.
PR review follow-up tightens an existing route_api_agent_pages_deploy_post row by updating auth_route_permissions after the seed insert.
CI follow-up restores the passwordless preview KV and D1 bindings to provisioned resources after Cloudflare rejected unprovisioned resource IDs.

Mural OAuth state signing now prefers the dedicated MURAL_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET, the Worker config marks it required and deployment workflows pass it to production, preview and passwordless preview Workers.

Codex review follow-up passes Worker authContext through the Mural journal sync safe-tag wrapper, injects the authenticated user id into the cloned request body and returns not_authenticated when no authenticated user reaches the wrapper.
Codex review follow-up runs 0025_security_review_route_permissions.sql during production, preview and passwordless preview deployments so existing D1 route rows are tightened.

validationEvidence.notes: Exited 0 with existing warning-only lint debt.

validationEvidence.summary: Covered proxy CORS/CSRF, diagnostics, deployment permission, Mural OAuth state/token ownership and production/preview configuration contracts.
16 tests passed after PR review and CI follow-up fixes.
npm run format:check, npm run lint, npm run trace:coverage, npm test and npm run validate passed after the Codex comment and preview deployment fixes.
Security hardening and Mural route-state contracts passed after adding MURAL_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET to code and deployment workflows.

MURAL_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET and RESEARCHOPS_AUTH_SECRET are present in GitHub repository secrets and both repo-backed Cloudflare Workers: rops-api and rops-api-passwordless-preview. Secret values were not printed.
Focused tests passed for Mural journal sync auth-context propagation, safe-tag wrapper uid injection and deployment workflow execution of the security route-permission migration.
Follow-up formatting check passed after Codex review fixes.

validationEvidence.notes: Exited 0 with existing warning-only lint debt.

validationEvidence.summary: 305 tests, 305 pass, 0 fail after rerunning sequentially.
Reran after a full-suite attempt overlapped with generated CSS work; the isolated asset-path test passed.
Repository validation passed after the Codex review fixes.

residualRisks: Passwordless preview storage remains on the provisioned KV and D1 bindings until separate preview resources are created and verified in Cloudflare.
Operators should review the first production retention run after deployment.
Mural OAuth state now uses MURAL_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET when configured and keeps RESEARCHOPS_AUTH_SECRET as a compatibility fallback.
The deployment migration fix updates future deployments; operators should confirm the next deployment log shows 0025_security_review_route_permissions.sql applied successfully.
